content.full_text:
May 7, 1975
                                                    75-05-132


Mr. William R. Johnson, Secretary
State Public Utilities Commission
350 McAllister Street
San Francisco, California 94102

Dear Mr. Johnson:

                    Re: Request for Opinion
                        No. 75-103

     Thank you for your memorandum of April 21, 1975
requesting an opinion with respect to the duties of
Vernon U. Sturgeon, President, under the Political
Reform Act.

     Since your request raises no substantial question
involving interpretation of the Political Reform Act,
your request for a formal opinion is denied. No question would arise under Government Code Section 87100
unless a matter came before the Public Utilities
Commission which would materially affect the Electric
Power Research Institute. In any such case, Mr. Sturgeon
would be required to disqualify himself. Of course
express no opinion as to whether this activity is permissible under Government Code Section 19251, which
prohibits incompatible activities.

     Although no formal opinion will be issued, I hope
the foregoing answers your questions and will be sufficiently helpful. If you have any further questions,
I urge you to contact a member of the Commission staff.

                              Sincerely,


                              Daniel H. Lowenstein
                              Chairman

DHL:cjb

State of California

Memorandum

To : Mr. Daniel Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
926 J Street
Sacramento

From : Public Utilities Commission—San Francisco

Date : April 21, 1975

Request No.: YD 10-
Date Received: 4-23-75
Response Due.: 5-7-75

Subject: [illegible] R. Johnson
William R. Johnson
Secretary

The President of the California Public Utilities Commission,
Vernon L. Sturgeon, has recently accepted an appointment to the
Advisory Council of the Electric Power Research Institute (EPRI)
located in Palo Alto, California. Enclosed is a copy of a
brochure setting forth the objectives, structure, funding and
research priorities of EPRI. Also enclosed is a copy of the
EPRI bylaws.

Compensation is paid to members of the Advisory Council on the
basis of $200 per meeting plus travel and other expenses incurred.
It is President Sturgeon's plan to have EPRI pay all such monies
to the general fund of the State of California. He would then
submit a standard State of California travel expense claim to
recover only those expenses approved under Board of Control
rules.

President Sturgeon requests that your Commission review the
enclosed material and render an opinion, as provided for in
Section 83114 of the Political Reform Act, as to whether any
violation of the conflict of interest prohibitions would occur
under the course of action set forth above.

Enclosures
